reason: A succeeded implement step completed the requested job chaining feature end-to-end, and independent review/test steps confirmed the required chain behavior plus successful `go build ./...` and `go test ./...`.
evidence: Step 1 (`implement`) has `status: succeeded` and reports changes to all required areas: domain types, state store persistence, orchestrator chaining/advancement, MCP tools, tests, and docs.
Step 1 reports verification commands `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` both passed.

Step 3 (`test`) independently verified required checks: first goal starts immediately, later goals stay pending until advanced, `done` advances, final `done` marks chain `done`, `failed`/`blocked` marks chain `failed`, and MCP start/status expose persisted chain state.
Step 2 (`review`) found no correctness issues and explicitly confirmed advancement occurs only from the evaluator-approved completion path after job status reaches `done`.

Required artifacts named in the verification contract are covered in the worker/test summaries, including `internal/domain/types.go`, `internal/store/state_store.go`, `internal/orchestrator/service.go`, `internal/mcp/server.go`, their tests, and updated docs.
